DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE AS TO CROSS COMPLIANT FILED BY COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY ON JAN-22-2013; DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE AS TO CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY ON FEB-28-2013.
DEMAND FOR JURY FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAGE, JOHN DBA STELLAR CONSTRUCTION
DECLARATION OF FRANCIS X. DOHERTY FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAGE, JOHN DBA STELLAR CONSTRUCTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY AND ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY ("CSIC"). THE COMPLAINT PLEADS THAT CSIC IS AN INSURING ENTITY, AND IT CANNOT BE DETERMINED FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT OR ITS EXHIBITS THAT CSIC, WHICH IS INCLUDED ON THE SIGNATURE PAGE OF THE POLICY, DID NOT INSURE PLAINTIFF. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ARGONAUT, BECAUSE ITS INCLUSION IS INCONSISTENT WITH THE FACE OF EXHIBIT 2 TO THE COMPLAINT AND IS UNSUPPORTED BY ANY OTHER CHARGING ALLEGATIONS THAT COULD SUPPORT ITS OBLIGATION UNDER THE CONTRACT. THE OBJECTION TO THE DOHERTY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-20-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
OPPOSITION OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY'S CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION OR OTHER LEGAL ENTITY
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-11-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN PAGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF-CROSS DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, WHICH SEEKS A DECLARATION REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S INSURANCE POLICY, ARISES FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 426.16 AND THE CASE LAW. SEE CITY OF COTATI V. CASHMAN, (2002) 29 CAL.4TH 69. THE MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS, AND DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO FILE A MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS. SEE CCP 425.16(C)(1). THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION OR OTHER LEGAL ENTITY

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY & COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS - PER CCP 425.16(C) AND THIS COURT'S ORDER OF APRIL 11, 2013 IS GRANTED IN PART. COLONY'S REQUEST FOR $5739.61 FOR OPPOSING THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION ARE REASONABLE. THE HOURLY RATES ARE REASONABLE, AS IS THE TIME EXPENDED, ALL SUBSTANTIATED BY MR. CHARLSTON'S DECLARATIONS. THE FACT THAT THE OPPOSING PAPERS WERE SHORT AND TO THE POINT IS NOT DISPOSITIVE. COLONY IS AWARDED $2000 IN REASONABLE FEES FOR MAKING THIS MOTION TO RECOVER FEES. THE TOTAL FEE AWARD S $7,739.61. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUZANNE BROWN CROW OF DOHERTY GEROGESON LLP, TEL. NO. 455-0265 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JEFFREY A. CHARLSTON OF CHARLSTON, REVICH & WOLITZ LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 551-7010 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY & COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs - Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013 is Granted in part. Colony's request for $5739.61 for opposing the anti-SLAPP motion are reasonable. The hourly rates are reasonable, as is the time expended, all substantiated by Mr. Charlston's declarations. The fact that the opposing papers were short and to the point is not dispositive. Colony is awarded $2000 in reasonable fees for making this motion to recover fees. The total fee award s $7,739.61. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-26-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SUZANNE CROW, ESQ., 415.455.0261, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN PAGE; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN PAGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff-Cross Defendant has not met its burden of showing that the cross-complaint, which seeks a declaration regarding plaintiff's insurance policy, arises from petitioning activity within the meaning of CCP 426.16 and the case law. See City of Cotati v. Cashman, (2002) 29 Cal.4th 69. The motion is frivolous, and Defendant has leave to file a motion for attorney's fees and costs. See CCP 425.16(c)(1). THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER AFTER HEARING. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Continued To Apr-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN PAGE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is continued on the court's own motion April 10, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY AND ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The demurrer is overruled as to Colony Specialty Insurance Company ("CSIC"). The complaint pleads that CSIC is an insuring entity, and it cannot be determined from the face of the complaint or its exhibits that CSIC, which is included on the signature page of the policy, did not insure Plaintiff. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Argonaut, because its inclusion is inconsistent with the face of Exhibit 2 to the Complaint and is unsupported by any other charging allegations that could support its obligation under the contract. The objection to the Doherty Declaration is sustained. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)
